steps.title: Identify the sample source and method
steps.content: The poll was conducted on the cable show's website, accessible only to people viewing that specific show. Respondents were self-selected—only those motivated enough to respond participated. This is a voluntary response sample, not a random sample of the US population.

steps.title: Explain why non-random sampling doesn't represent the population
steps.content: For poll results to reflect the entire US population, respondents must be randomly or probability-based sampled. This poll attracted only show viewers motivated to respond—not a random cross-section of America. The show's audience likely has distinct demographics and political leanings, so the 28% 'Yes' and 70% 'No' represent that specific audience, not the general population.

steps.title: Evaluate choices and confirm answer
steps.content:
• Choice A: 28% + 70% = 98%, leaving 2%. This small gap doesn't invalidate results—some respondents may have abstained or chosen 'no opinion.'
  • Choice C: There's no reason to expect a 50–50 split; the outcome reflects the actual views of the participating sample.
  • Choice D: Even with more time, the core problem remains: the sample consists only of show viewers, not the general US population.
  • Choice B: This directly identifies the fundamental issue—respondents were not a random sample of the US population; they were self-selected from the show's audience.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
